title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence
description: A team says it built HTTP from scratch because one request works, but it cannot separate application semantics, wire syntax, transport behavior, implementation policy, or user and operator evidence. Learners cumulatively produce protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define user, client, operator, security, accessibility, interoperability, and recovery evidence for a protocol implementation.
Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.
Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.
